Regular Military Every affiliation operates standing military; and the char- acters are part 0f it strict chain 0i command provides less freedom than some types of campaigns, but as member of Varge ana Organized army supply and repair are less of prob- Tem Characters can worry less about paying the bills and con- centrate on accomplishing the mission at hand This setup ideal for NOvice gamemasters and ones with limited time since the NPC commanders hand on spe- cific Orders (your adventures which the player characters are obliged to follow: Since the mission can stray little from the patn you sel. helps keep the players track and forces you t0 deal with fewer surdrises. NON-PLAYER CHARACTERS With the exception of the players characters, every person the BattleTech universe is non-player character (NPCE Inat the gamemaster can use as the cast of nis stories. It is up t0 the gamemaster create and portray each of these characters believable and enjoyable manner. Populating campaign with interesting NPCs is no small task so this section devoted to helping gamemasters get started It includes sample NPCs the gamemaster can use as as well as guidelines for creating new NPCs and bringing them t0 llife
Media Tne interstellar media big business in the thirty first cen- tury. and your characters are tield reporters investigating the nottest stories In tne war-torn BattleTech universe: more often than not those stories involve combat. putting you in the linc fire t0 get t0 the bottom of the story: Once you ve gotten close the scoop; it" only small step to becoming personally involved Almost ay character type can become involved in medla campaign but the best-suited are charismatic types such as Mer chants politicians and anyone with decent Acting or Negotiation Skill:  Technicians are always needed t0 keep tne cameras and vehicles rolling; and reporting from dangerous zones requires the addition of security specialists, most otten ex-military.
Mercenaries Soldiers-for-hire have existed nearly long as warfare itself, and never has the ground been more fertile for the enter prising mercenary unit: Everyone but the Clans is actively hiring mercs these days_ most often in anticipation conflict , but sometimes t0 use In missions where deniability must be main- tained. As result; mercs find themselves in every kind of sit- uation, trom pointless garrison duty t0 high-riSk raids mercenary campaign naturally fits well with MechWarriors other soldiers; However; there is no shortage of support duties be pertormed; irom medics to technicians t0 aircraft spacecratt pilots Some mercenary units are remarkably un: military character llowing civilians and soldiers to work side by side with Iittle the way 0f formal chain of command: get- Ung the job done all that mallers
MAJOR NpCS Wnether the players archenemy; major personage the Inner  Spnere critical non piayer member 0lthe Dlayers tearn . supporting characters are often essential t0 good story- Unlike the random generic characters that come ad go count- less limes in the course of an adventure major NPCs are fully developed The only difference between major NPCs ad the player characters iS that the gamemaster controls the NPCs. The fairest way of creating these important NPCs simply t0 use the samc character generation rules the Dlayers did at the start of the campaign If the gamemaster doesn nave the time to go through the whole Life Path system. simply assigning appropriate skills and traits certainly acceptable_ using the Sample Characters (p_ 601and tne player characters tnem selves a5 guide they are intended to be major challenge_ augmenting these NPCs beyond the typical starting evel may be necessary from time time which can be accomplished simply by boost ing skill bonuses and giving them expensive equipment: archenemy; for example May have nideous amount ot previ" ously earned and used Experience Points boosting her skills and Attributes t0 the point where the player characters cannot hope defeat ner easily (and certainly not the tirst encountcr). On the other hand_ another major NPC might be veteran tecnnician who has about tne same experience as the player characters nave earned_ only devoted specific arcas that make nim an invaluable addition t0 tne ranks but not So much that he can substitute for player.
Pirates and Bandits The characters raiders preying the Successor Stales and Clans for supplies survival or just kicks_ Cnaracters will be forced to deal with limited resources and the hazards of being wanted criminals. While they may enjoy some freedom from reprisal the isolation 0t space. eventually the long arm of the Iaw (whether Clan or Inner Sphere wIll catch up with them: potential variant of this hook tor the characters freedom fighters using their abilities and whatever resources they can scrape together t0 cause problems for tne rivae affilia tion thal nas captured their nomeworld; Outlaws some; they are heroes t0 the subjugated people of their home_ One further option to have the characters playing noble thieves_ classic Rooin Hood style Iney raid from the rich and powerful and give t0 the poor Of the surrounding worlds This may prove quite risky: but the rewards_ both material and spiri" tual;, are worth iL.
